facts.statement: Parker-Hannifin is a Fortune 250 global leader in motion and control technologies, serving diversified industrial and aerospace markets for more than a century.
The Win Strategy is Parker's business system that defines the goals and initiatives that drive growth, transformation and success.
Parker-Hannifin is classified under SIC code for Miscellaneous Fabricated Metal Products and trades on the NYSE under ticker symbol PH.
Revenue was $19.065 billion for FY2025, filed August 22, 2025.
Net income was $1,315,605,000 for FY2024.
Operating cash flow was $2.98 billion for FY2025, representing 19% of sales.
facts.source: SEC company facts summary (https://data.sec.gov/api/xbrl/companyfacts/CIK0000076334.); DEF 14A filed 2025-09-19 (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/76334/000130817925000603/ph-20250919.htm)

facts.statement: Research and development expense was $258 million for FY2025.
As of FY2025, stockholders' equity was $12.072 billion, long-term debt was $8.412 billion, and cash and equivalents were $422 million.

Parker-Hannifin operates in two reportable segments: Diversified Industrial and Aerospace Systems, with Diversified Industrial further divided into Motion Systems, Flow and Process Control, and Filtration and Engineered Materials groups.

The Aerospace Systems segment is further broken down into Commercial Original Equipment Manufacturer, Commercial Aftermarket, Defense Original Equipment Manufacturer, and Defense Aftermarket categories.
Segment operating margin was a record at 23.0% in fiscal year 2025.
Earnings per share as reported were a record at $27.12 in fiscal year 2025.

Net sales for FY2025 were $13,406 million in North America and $6,444 million internationally, totaling $19,850 million; no country other than the United States represents greater than 10 percent of consolidated sales.
Parker-Hannifin increased its annual dividend per share for the 69th consecutive year in fiscal year 2025, including a 10 percent increase in the quarterly cash dividend.
Parker-Hannifin repurchased $1.6 billion of shares in fiscal year 2025.
Parker-Hannifin announced an agreement to acquire Curtis Instruments, Inc. in fiscal year 2025 to further strengthen its electrification portfolio.

Parker-Hannifin acquired Meggitt plc, with the acquisition referenced as having closed around September 12, 2022, and involving customer relationships, technology-based intangible assets, and trade names.
During FY2025, Parker-Hannifin divested its Composites and Fuel Containment business and Non-Core Filtration business within the Diversified Industrial segment, both in November 2024.
Jennifer A. Parmentier serves as Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Parker-Hannifin Corporation.
Todd M. Leombruno serves as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer (Principal Financial Officer) of Parker-Hannifin Corporation.

The Board of Directors includes Jennifer A. Parmentier (Chairman and CEO), Denise Russell Fleming, Lance M. Fritz, Linda A. Harty, Kevin A. Lobo, E. Jean Savage, Joseph Scaminace, Laura K. Thompson, James R. Verrier, James L. Wainscott, and Beth A. Wozniak as nominees for terms expiring in 2026.
91% of Parker-Hannifin's Board is independent, with average tenure of 8.6 years and average age of 62 years; 73% of directors are gender, racially, or ethnically diverse.
The CEO's target total direct compensation is 9% fixed and 91% at-risk, while other Named Executive Officers have an average mix of 20% fixed and 80% at-risk.

As of June 30, 2025, 3,783,595 securities were to be issued upon exercise of outstanding options, warrants and rights at a weighted-average exercise price of $288.60, with 17,153,703 securities remaining available for future issuance.

LTIP awards payouts are determined based on achieving an average return on average equity of four percent or an average free cash flow margin of four percent, with maximum payout of 200 percent if achieved.
As of July 31, 2025, The Vanguard Group owned 8.26% of shares, State Street Corporation owned 7.74%, and BlackRock, Inc. owned 6.74%.
A fire at Parker-Hannifin's plant in Saegertown, Pennsylvania in February 2025 resulted in deductible and retained liability expense recorded in other (income) expense, net.

Parker-Hannifin has equity method investments in joint venture companies with 50 percent or less ownership, receiving cash dividends of $167 million in FY2025, $148 million in FY2024, and $114 million in FY2023.
Management concluded that Parker-Hannifin's internal control over financial reporting was effective as of June 30, 2025, with Deloitte & Touche LLP issuing an attestation report.
Director retirement is mandatory at Parker-Hannifin after reaching age 72, with no exceptions or conditions.
Parker-Hannifin has annual election of all directors, majority voting with resignation policy for uncontested elections, and proxy access permitted for eligible shareholders.

Parker-Hannifin does not offer employment agreements to executives, does not grant stock options or SARs with exercise prices below fair market value, does not reprice options or SARs, and maintains anti-hedging and anti-pledging policies for directors and executive officers.
On September 5, 2025, 126,544,072 shares of Parker-Hannifin common stock were outstanding and entitled to vote at the annual meeting.
